_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-16 Patient Restraint (Revised 3/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
level_display: Procedure (All EMS Levels — pharmacological requires Paramedic)
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-16: Patient Restraint — MDHHS Approved 3/17/2025
purpose: Govern the use of physical and pharmacological restraint in combative or dangerous patients. Restraint follows an escalation ladder: verbal de-escalation → soft restraints → pharmacological. Verbal de-escalation continues throughout all phases of care.

sections.title: Escalation Ladder
points: STEP 1 — VERBAL DE-ESCALATION: always the first intervention; continues throughout ALL phases of care regardless of escalation level
STEP 2 — SOFT RESTRAINTS: when verbal de-escalation is insufficient to ensure safety
STEP 3 — PHARMACOLOGICAL RESTRAINT: only when soft restraints pose a safety risk OR are ineffective

sections.title: Soft Restraint Guidelines
points: Soft restraints may be applied to the backboard or stretcher ONLY
NEVER place the patient in a PRONE position while restrained
NEVER secure restraints to the vehicle or to an immovable object
Check neurovascular status of restrained extremities CONTINUOUSLY
Document neurovascular checks in PCR

sections.title: Prohibited Restraint Techniques
points: Hard plastic restraint ties or zip ties
Any restraint device that cannot be immediately removed in an emergency
Backboard sandwiching (placing a board on top of the patient)
Hands and feet secured behind the patient's back
Hog-tie restraint (connecting wrists and ankles behind the patient's back)
Any device or position that restricts the patient's ability to breathe
KETAMINE is NOT authorized for patient restraint without direct online Medical Control direction

sections.title: Pharmacological Restraint
points: Paramedic only
Indicated when soft restraints pose a safety risk to the patient or crew, or when soft restraints are ineffective
Contact Medical Control PRIOR to pharmacological restraint UNLESS there is an extreme emergency requiring immediate intervention
MIDAZOLAM 0.1 mg/kg IM or IN:
— Adult maximum: 10 mg (consider lower dose for geriatric patients due to increased sensitivity)
— Pediatric maximum: 5 mg IM
Monitor airway, ventilation, SpO2 continuously after administration
Document contact with Medical Control, indication for pharmacological restraint, and patient response

sections.title: Law Enforcement Restraints
points: If a patient is in law enforcement restraints (e.g., handcuffs): a law enforcement officer MUST accompany the patient to the hospital
OR: apply appropriate EMS soft restraints if safe to do so and remove law enforcement restraints
Document presence of law enforcement restraints and the name of the law enforcement officer accompanying the patient

key_drugs.name: Midazolam (Pharmacological Restraint)
key_drugs.dose: 0.1 mg/kg IM or IN; adult max 10 mg (lower for geriatric); pediatric max 5 mg IM
key_drugs.indication: Pharmacological restraint when soft restraints are unsafe or ineffective
key_drugs.contraindications: Contact Medical Control prior unless extreme emergency; Ketamine NOT authorized without online MC
key_drugs.notes: Monitor airway and SpO2 continuously. Consider lower dose for geriatric patients. Contact MC prior unless extreme emergency.

deterioration_flags: Prone restraint — PROHIBITED; reposition to supine or lateral; monitor breathing continuously
Airway compromise with restrained patient — immediately reposition; release restraints if needed to manage airway; airway takes absolute priority
SpO2 decreasing after midazolam — assist ventilation; monitor airway; have BVM ready
scope_notes: Verbal de-escalation: all levels; continues throughout care
Soft restraints: all levels; backboard/stretcher only; never prone; never to vehicle; continuous neurovascular checks
Pharmacological restraint: Paramedic only; contact MC prior unless extreme emergency
Midazolam: 0.1 mg/kg IM/IN; adult max 10 mg; peds max 5 mg IM
Ketamine: NOT authorized for restraint without online MC direction
Law enforcement restraints: officer must accompany or EMS soft restraints applied
